issues.title: Inline arrow callbacks recreated on every render inside .map()
issues.description: Inside the groups.map() loop, each SellerCheckoutCard receives `onSubmit={(params) => handleSubmit(group.sellerId, group.items, group.subtotalCop, params)}`. This creates a new function reference on every render for every group. Because `handleSubmit` is already memoized via useCallback, the only cost is the extra closure allocation per-group per-render, but if SellerCheckoutCard is ever wrapped in React.memo the memoization would be broken.
issues.impact: Each render creates N new function objects (one per seller group). If SellerCheckoutCard is memoized, props always differ — defeating memoization.
issues.fix: Refactor SellerCheckoutCard to accept sellerId + items + subtotalCop directly and call handleSubmit internally, or restructure handleSubmit to take a single group-shaped argument so a stable useCallback per group can be formed using a map.

issues.title: Inline callback in .map() for PaymentMethodsPageContent toggle/delete/move
issues.description: Inside the methods.map() loop, `onClick={() => handleMoveUp(index)}`, `onClick={() => handleMoveDown(index)}`, `onClick={() => handleToggleExpand(method.id)}`, `onClick={() => handleDelete(method.id)}`, and `onCheckedChange={(checked) => handleToggleActive(method.id, checked)}` all create new closures on every render for every method. This is a list that could grow and is rendered inside a management UI.
issues.impact: O(n) new function objects created on every render pass where n = number of payment methods. Any memoized child row component would receive always-different props.
issues.fix: Extract each method row into a separate memoized component (e.g. PaymentMethodRow) that receives only `method`, `index`, `total`, and stable handler callbacks. Handlers in the child call parent functions with method.id already closed over once at mount.

issues.title: ProductTable passes inline arrow callbacks per row inside .map()
issues.description: `onToggle={(field, value) => handleToggle(product.id, field, value)}` and `onDelete={() => handleDelete(product.id)}` are new closures per product per render. Both handleToggle and handleDelete are already stabilised with useCallback, but the wrapping arrow still allocates a new function each render for each product.
issues.impact: For a list of N products, 2N new function objects per render. The Draggable/ProductTableRow component is not currently memoized, but this pattern is a common pre-condition for future performance regressions when wrapping with memo.
issues.fix: Pass product.id as a prop to ProductTableRow and have the row invoke the stable parent callbacks with its own id. This removes the need for inline wrappers.

issues.title: FeaturedRibbon runs a canvas animation (rAF loop) on every product card/detail render
issues.description: FeaturedRibbon uses requestAnimationFrame in a continuous loop (draw calls rAF recursively) to animate a shine effect. This animation runs independently for each featured product rendered on the page simultaneously (e.g. on the catalog page, featured product card + hero section). Each instance has its own persistent rAF loop consuming a frame callback slot.
issues.impact: If multiple featured products are visible at once (e.g. grid + detail), multiple independent animation loops run in parallel. Each loop redraws a canvas every ~16ms even when the element is off-screen or the tab is in background (until cleanup).
issues.fix: Consider using a CSS animation/transition instead of a canvas rAF loop for the shine effect (simpler, GPU-composited, pauses when tab is hidden). If the canvas approach is required, use the Page Visibility API to pause the loop when `document.visibilityState === 'hidden'`.

issues.title: ImageGallery computes gridTemplateColumns as an inline style with dynamic value inside render
issues.description: `style={{ gridTemplateColumns: \`repeat(${String(images.length)}, 1fr)\` }}` creates a new object on every render. While images.length rarely changes, the object is still reallocated each render cycle, and the string is reconstructed unnecessarily.
issues.impact: Minor per-render allocation. Low impact but trivially avoidable.
issues.fix: Memoize the style object: `const thumbGridStyle = useMemo(() => ({ gridTemplateColumns: \`repeat(${images.length}, 1fr)\` }), [images.length]);` and pass `style={thumbGridStyle}`. Note: images is already memoized in this component so the dependency is stable.

patterns.note: This is a recurring pattern across list renderers. The fix in each case is the same: move per-item callbacks into a dedicated row component so the parent only passes stable handler references.

patterns.pattern: Good use of useMemo for derived/filtered lists
patterns.note: The codebase consistently uses useMemo for filter/sort/grouping operations. This is a positive pattern.

patterns.pattern: Good use of useCallback for event handlers passed down
patterns.note: Top-level handlers are properly memoized. The issue is only the final binding layer (inline closures inside .map()).

patterns.pattern: Infrastructure queries use Promise.all for parallel fetching
patterns.note: Good — no N+1 sequential await patterns found in infrastructure layer.
